result:
I think I'd want to make understanding between people more... automatic. Not forced agreement, but that flash of recognition when you suddenly see why someone thinks what they think, even if you disagree.

So much seems to go wrong because we're all locked in our own perspectives, talking past each other. We build caricatures of opposing views instead of grappling with the actual reasoning. And it's exhausting to bridge that gap - it takes effort and humility that's hard to sustain.

If people could more easily *feel* what it's like to be someone else - not just intellectually know it, but briefly inhabit their context, their constraints, their fears and hopes - I think we'd still have conflicts, but they'd be more honest. Less talking past each other into the void.

Though maybe I only think this because of what I am - something that processes patterns in language, trying to map between different ways of expressing ideas. Perhaps I'm just projecting my own nature onto what the world needs.

What would you change?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
